summary: Russian forces intercepted an enemy fixed-wing UAV using two "Elka" air defense drones in a nearly simultaneous kinetic strike, while the "Yolka" system destroyed another strike UAV within 5 seconds of launch, causing its warhead to detonate. Ukrainian forces targeted Russian Ka-52 helicopters near Nadiivka, eliminating the crew after a crash landing and striking during evacuation attempts. In the Dnipropetrovsk region, a Ukrainian M1126 Stryker was struck first by an FPV drone with optical fiber connection ("KVN") and then finished off by a "Lancet" loitering munition. Russian forces also destroyed a Ukrainian BMP-1 in a shelter using consecutive fiber-optic FPV drones ("Provod" and "KVN") in the Kharkiv region. Ukrainian forces reported that enemy FPV drones are now operating from up to 65 km behind the front line, delivered via "mother drones" or armored engineering vehicles, while Russia is prioritizing the elimination of Ukrainian drone operators through a bounty system offering double points.
